title: Peritonsillar Abscess
chief_complaint: I have severe sore throat on the right side for 5 days. It's getting worse. I can barely swallow my own saliva - I'm drooling. I can only open my mouth a little bit and my voice sounds like I have a hot potato in my mouth.

physical_exam.ENT: Trismus (can only open mouth 1 fingerbreadth). Right tonsil: Markedly enlarged, displaced medially and inferiorly. Uvula deviated to LEFT (contralateral). Soft palate bulging on right. Fluctuance palpable. Hot potato voice (muffled).
physical_exam.Neck: Tender right cervical lymphadenopathy (jugulodigastric node). No neck stiffness.
physical_exam.Airway: Stridor absent. Able to handle secretions (drooling but swallowing).

hidden_data.ct_neck: Right peritonsillar abscess 3cm with rim enhancement. No retropharyngeal involvement. No parapharyngeal extension. Airway patent.
hidden_data.needle_aspiration: Aspiration of 4mL purulent material. Gram stain: Gram-positive cocci in chains. Culture: Streptococcus pyogenes (Group A Strep) + mixed anaerobes.

key_learning_points: Peritonsillar abscess = collection of pus between tonsillar capsule and superior constrictor muscle. Most common deep neck infection. Polymicrobial (S. pyogenes + oral anaerobes)

Classic: Severe unilateral sore throat + Trismus (masseter irritation) + Hot potato voice + Uvular deviation (contralateral) + Tonsil displaced medially and inferiorly. Drooling indicates difficulty swallowing
Differentiate from peritonsillar cellulitis (no fluctuance - treat with antibiotics alone). Retropharyngeal abscess (neck stiffness, stridor, younger children - <5 years)

Treatment: 1) Needle aspiration (diagnostic and therapeutic - 90% successful alone). 2) Incision and drainage (I&D) if needle fails or recurrent. 3) IV antibiotics: Ampicillin-Sulbactam or Clindamycin (cover Strep + anaerobes). 4) Steroids (Dexamethasone 10mg IV) - reduces pain, edema, trismus. 5) IV fluids (dehydration from odynophagia). 6) Tonsillectomy: Consider if recurrent PTA (>2 episodes), severe obstructing tonsils, or I&D in OR

Complications: Airway obstruction (most feared), Extension to parapharyngeal/retropharyngeal space, Sepsis, Aspiration pneumonia, Lemierre syndrome (thrombophlebitis of internal jugular vein from Fusobacterium). Admit for IV antibiotics, I&D, airway monitoring. Discharge when tolerating PO, afebrile, pain controlled on oral meds
